blurb: An ultra-hardy berry shrub that pairs spring corymbs of pink-anthered white flowers with brilliant red fall foliage and glossy black antioxidant-packed fruit.
water: Moderate; adaptable but not truly drought-tolerant — wants supplemental water on the dry Front Range
spread: Suckers freely into a multi-stem clonal thicket over time
seasons: Pink-anthered white flower clusters May–Jun; glossy dark-green summer foliage; blackish-purple berries Aug–Sep; brilliant wine-red fall color; bare twiggy frame in winter
wildlife: Spring flowers feed bees & pollinators; ripe berries draw birds (cedar waxwings, robins) into fall
toxic: Non-toxic to people and pets; the fruit is edible (very astringent raw)
origin: Eastern North America (NE US & Canada — not a Colorado native)
habitat: Bogs, swamp edges, moist woods and dry uplands — wide moisture range on acid to neutral soils
